g.text: Kubernetes KMS API v2 - k8s.io/kms/apis/v2 & KMS Plugin for PKCS #11
After a kubernetes reboot, k8s sends both a first StatusRequest to know which key id is active.
k8s generates a new DEK seed and a corresponding EncryptionRequest in case the key id changed.
k8s sends as many DecryptRequests as their are encrypted DEK seeds in ETCD; preferably it should
not be more than 2 different DEKs at the same time. This happens during a key rotation.
Case: KEK Key Id from the k8s-kms-plugin is the same than before k8s reboot (same KEK)
KeyId:<remote KEK ID string, the same than before k8s reboot>}
[For active KEK and all the old KEK Keys (As many different Key Ids) that still exist in the ETCD DB, do a DecryptRequest.]
decrypt DEK seed with remote KEK
generate DEK using decrypted DEK seed from cache
Kubernetes KMS v2 & k8s-kms-plugin Figure 1 of 1

CKA_ID (Byte array) PKCS #11 Key identifier for public/private key pairs
